data.title: Stock index futures muted as China slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods; PPI falls
data.description:
Stock index futures were largely flat on Friday, as the U.S.-China trade war seemed to heat up, with China further retaliating against President Donald Trump's tariffs.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were unchanged, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.41%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.83%.

China has announced an increase in tariffs on U.S. goods to 125%, up from the previous rate of 84%, effective April 12, and stated it will disregard any additional tariff hikes announced by Washington moving forward.

Headline and core producer prices unexpectedly fell M/M in March.

Banks' Q1 earnings brought some cheer to investors, as JPMorgan (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC) and Bank of New York Mellon (BK) all beat their bottom line estimates. 

Wall Street's euphoria disappeared on Thursday as market participants were back in selloff mode.

"The main driver of the renewed market pressure was an increased focus on the US-China escalation, with yesterday’s market downturn accelerating after a White House clarification that total tariffs on China would now be 145% rather than 125%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

While this difference is negligible in any practical economic sense, the market reaction showed an increased sensitivity to the risks of a disorderly economic decoupling between the world’s two largest economies that we highlighted yesterday, Sidorov added.

Trump’s administration clarified the 125% tax on imports from China was 145%. This increase will not shift demand patterns. For investors, this raises policy competence questions again. A well-planned trade tax would consider costs. Tax rates set seemingly at random question whether costs are properly considered, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent said that they saw nothing unusual in yesterday’s market moves. The 30-year Treasury yield experienced the biggest increase since 1982, equities are falling, the dollar is falling, and gold hit an all-time high. Economists might consider this pattern not entirely usual," Donovan added. 

The March consumer sentiment report will land later in the day and is forecasted to come in at 54.

data.title: China tariffs exempt U.S. semiconductor companies that outsource manufacturing: report
data.description:
China's import tariffs on U.S. goods provide an exemption for U.S. semiconductor firms that outsource their manufacturing, according to a post by the China Semiconductor Industry Association, on Friday.

"For all integrated circuits, whether packaged or unpackaged, the declared country of origin for import customs purchases is the location of the wafer fabrication plant," the CSIA posted on WeChat, according to Reuters. 

U.S. firms such as Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), who outsource manufacturing to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM), are exempted as China regulators classify these chips as originating from Taiwan, the report said.

"In contrast, chips made by Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN), ADI (NASDAQ:ADI), and ON Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) - which operate their own U.S.-based fabs - may be classified as U.S. origin and be liable for tariff rates of 84% or higher," the CSIA noted.

As a result, U.S. semiconductor stocks diverged during early Friday trading. Texas Instruments sank 5.5%, Intel declined 2%, Analog Devices inched down 0.5% and ON slipped 1.6%. Conversely, Qualcomm edged up 1% and AMD climbed 1.7%.

On Friday, China boosted its tariffs on U.S. imports to 125% from 84%, and stated it will disregard any additional tariff hikes announced by Washington moving forward. The White House on Thursday clarified that the cumulative tariff on China-produced goods coming into the U.S. will total 145%.

Earlier in the week, Trump issued a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs on more than 75 countries – excluding China.

data.title: Nio Strategic Metals announces debt conversion agreements
data.description:
• Nio Strategic Metals (TSXV:NIO:CA) intends to enter into shares for debt agreements to satisfy an aggregate of $495,000 of the firm's outstanding debt.
  • An aggregate of 11M common shares in the capital of the corporation at a deemed price of $0.045 per share are proposed to be issued.
  • Nio agreed on a share for debt conversion agreement with Nio-Metals Holdings LLC to convert the outstanding principal of a loan amount of $450,000 and to settle unpaid accrued services fees of $45,000 for services with four current directors and the CFO of the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Prospect Capital to acquire QC Holdings for an enterprise value of ~$115M
data.description:
Business development company Prospect Capital (NASDAQ:PSEC) announced on Friday the acquisition of the financial services provider QC Holdings (OTCPK:QCCO) for an enterprise value of ~$115M.

PSEC shares were +1.15% pre-market to $3.53.

The all-cash transaction puts the purchase price at $2.00 per share.

The definitive merger agreement has been approved by the board of directors of QC Holdings as well as the holders of a majority of its outstanding shares.

No other stockholder approval is required, but the acquisition is subject to the receipt of certain required regulatory approvals.

The transaction is expected to close in 40 to 60 days.

QC shares will cease trading on the OTC Pink Market post the completion of the merger.

data.title: Mixed Martial Arts gets $2M non-dilutive financing
data.description:
• Mixed Martial Arts (NYSE:MMA) said it has entered into a non-dilutive and unsecured $2M revolving loan agreement.
  • The loan will accrue interest at a rate of 14% per annum and will mature 12 months from issuance.
  • As part of the financing, Hugh Williams and Jonathan Hart resigned as directors of the firm, and Richard Paolone, Eric Corbett, and Angel Liriano were appointed as directors.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Q1 earnings
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) delivered a Q1 earnings beat on Friday as equities revenue surged Y/Y and client assets increased at its wealth and investment management businesses. Pretax profit margin grew to 31% in the quarter.

"Institutional Securities strong performance was led by our Markets business, with Equity reporting a record $4.1B in revenues," Chairman and CEO Ted Pick said. "Total client assets of $7.7T across Wealth and Investment Management were supported by $94B in net new assets."

Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) stock rose 0.4% in premarket trading.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.60, topping the average analyst estimate of $2.19, rose from $2.22 in Q4 2024 and $2.02 in Q1 2024.

Net revenue of $17.7B, beating the consensus estimate of $16.6B, climbed from $16.2B in the prior quarter and $15.1B a year ago.

Morgan Stanley’s (MS) net interest income of $2.35B, matching the Visible Alpha consensus, decreased from $2.55B in Q4 and increased from $1.80B in last year’s Q1.

Pretax profit margin of 31% vs. 30% in the previous quarter and 29% a year ago. Compensation and benefits, as a percentage of net revenue, was 42% compared with 39% in Q4 and 44% in Q1 2024.

Total non-compensation expenses were $4.54B, down from $4.91B in the prior quarter and up from $4.05B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $134M vs. $77M Visible Alpha consensus, rose from $115M in the previous quarter and -$0.6M a year ago.

Morgan Stanley's (MS) return on average tangible common equity of 23.0% strengthened from 20.2% in Q4 2024 and 19.7% in Q1 2024.

Revenue by segment:

  • Institutional Securities: $8.98B jumped 24% Q/Q and 28% Y/Y; investment banking revenue of $1.56B increased 8% Y/Y. Equity net revenue of $4.13B shot up 45% Y/Y, reflecting increases across all business lines and regions, especially Asia. Fixed income net revenue of $2.60B rose 5% Y/Y.

  • Wealth Management: $7.33B slipped 2% Q/Q and rose 6% Y/Y. Asset management revenue of $4.40B climbed from $3.83B a year ago on higher asset levels and the cumulative impact of positive fee-based flows.

  • Investment Management: $1.60B fell 5% Q/Q and 26% Y/Y. Asset management and related fees of $1.45B grew from $1.35B a year ago, on higher average AUM, primarily driven by higher market levels, the company said.

Conference call at 9:30 AM ET.

data.title: Intapp announces plan to acquire TermSheet
data.description:
• Intapp (NASDAQ:INTA) has signed an agreement to acquire TermSheet, a provider of software for real estate teams.
  • TermSheet, is an affiliate of Platform Ventures, a Kansas City-based investment firm. The transaction is expected to close within the next 45 days.
  • Together, Intapp DealCloud and TermSheet will deliver an advanced operating system that applies intelligence, so clients can better execute across the client lifecycle, improve returns, and foster firm growth.
  • “Bringing together Intapp DealCloud and TermSheet will deliver a more powerful operating system tailored to the complex needs of the commercial real estate industry and create an unparalleled team of industry experts.” said Erin Guinan, General Manager of DealCloud at Intapp.
